title: Error::non_exhaustive + structured variants is the right public API choice
description: Both wolftpm::Error and wolftpm-tss::Error use #[non_exhaustive] and structured variants (e.g., InvalidHashLen { got: usize }, Transport { code: i32 }). This is exactly right: #[non_exhaustive] allows adding variants without a semver break; structured variants preserve the actual values for programmatic handling without losing them in a String. A contributor might suggest simplifying to String-based error wrapping (simpler to write) — the structured approach should be defended because it lets callers match on variants and inspect values, which matters for error recovery and testing.
